# HedHog Lab v2 - AI Coding Agent Instructions

This file is intentionally compact and cross-cutting.
Domain execution details live in specialized agents under `.github/agents`.

## Canonical Language

- The canonical language for customization files is English.

## Core Non-Negotiables

1. Respect architecture boundaries.
- Business logic belongs in `libraries/*`.
- App entrypoints belong in `apps/*`.
- Shared adapters/utilities belong in `packages/*`.

2. Use workspace package imports.
- Prefer `@hed-hog/*` imports.
- Do not use deep relative imports across workspaces.

3. Keep changes minimal and compatible.
- Reuse existing patterns before introducing new variants.
- Avoid breaking changes unless explicitly requested.

4. Validate focused quality checks when feasible.
- Run targeted lint/test/build in the changed app/package.

5. Preserve frontend click affordance.
- In frontend UI, clickable rows/cards/list items must use `cursor-pointer` (or an equivalent component style that results in a pointer cursor).

6. Keep admin CRUD/list pages on the canonical layout.
- Use the first generated page under `apps/admin/src/app/(app)/(libraries)/<library>/<entity>/page.tsx` as the reference for admin list pages once one exists (this folder is empty in a fresh template checkout — it is generated by `hedhog dev create-library` / `assets-to-library`).
- The standard shell is: `PageHeader` -> KPI row (when useful) -> one flat toolbar row with `SearchBar` + filters on the left and the `Tabela/Cards` toggle on the right -> content -> `PaginationFooter`.
- Do not wrap the toolbar or the whole listing area in an extra parent `Card`, and avoid duplicate list titles/descriptions below `PageHeader` unless there is a clear product reason.

7. Standardize admin selector-plus-create flows.
- For searchable relation/entity selectors with inline creation in `apps/admin`, prefer the shared `EntityPicker` at `apps/admin/src/components/ui/entity-picker.tsx` (import path `@/components/ui/entity-picker`).
- Do not create new full `*FieldWithCreate` or `*SelectWithCreate` variants for the same interaction model.
- If a domain-specific name improves readability, keep it as a thin adapter over `EntityPicker` instead of duplicating the behavior.

8. Standardize paginated list page size persistence.
- For any page or component that exposes a user-facing page-size selector (via `PaginationFooter` or equivalent), manage `pageSize` state with `usePersistedPageSize` from `@/hooks/use-persisted-page-size` — never with plain `useState`.
- Always use `storageKey: 'pagination:global:pageSize'` so the preference is shared across all screens.
- `allowedValues` must match the `pageSizeOptions` array passed to `PaginationFooter` (default `[6, 12, 24, 48]` when not overridden).
- `defaultValue` must be a value that exists in `allowedValues`; when in doubt, use the first element.
- Do not persist page size for fixed, non-user-selectable values (`const pageSize = 10` with `onPageSizeChange={() => undefined}`).

9. Standardize admin form draft persistence and privacy.
- For admin create/edit forms that need draft recovery, use the shared hook at `apps/admin/src/hooks/use-form-draft.ts`.
- Keep drafts in localStorage with per-user isolation so the same user can recover work after involuntary auth interruption or relogin.
- Clear drafts on successful submit and purge all draft entries on manual logout.
- Never persist passwords, tokens, or raw file/blob payloads in drafts, and do not reimplement ad-hoc draft storage per form.

10. Shared components first (mandatory).
- Always prefer existing shared components over page-local implementations.
- For recurring UI flows (create/edit sheets, dialogs, pickers, and complex form sections), reuse or extend components in `apps/admin/src/components/**`.
- Do not duplicate full create/edit flow logic directly inside page files when a shared abstraction is feasible.
- If a temporary page-local implementation is required for delivery, extract it to a shared component before finishing the task.

11. Use `FileTypeIcon` for all file type displays.
- Whenever a filename, MIME type, or file record from the `file` table is displayed, use `FileTypeIcon` from `@/components/file-type-icon` (import path: `apps/admin/src/components/file-type-icon.tsx`).
- Pass `filename` (the original filename string) and optionally `mimeType` (MIME type string or LMS custom tags like `video_original`/`video_profile:*`).
- Never use raw Lucide icons (`FileText`, `FileImage`, `FileIcon`, etc.) as stand-ins for file type indicators.
- `FileTypeIcon` automatically falls back to a generic Lucide icon when the CDN is unavailable.

12. Standardize all new admin Sheets with resize behavior.
- Every new Sheet in `apps/admin` must use `ResizableSheetContent` (not raw `SheetContent`) for desktop resize support and consistent behavior.
- Always provide a stable `sheetId` so width is persisted per user/sheet in localStorage.
- Keep mobile behavior full-width (`100%` / `100vw`) via the shared resizable sheet wrapper.
- Prefer `defaultWidth`, `minWidth`, and `maxWidth` values aligned with existing screens; avoid ad-hoc width logic.

## Mandatory Workflows

1. Prisma migration-first workflow.
- Never manually edit `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`.
- Never run `hedhog dev apply` in this `hub` repository.
- Never run commands that reset/recreate the project, database, schema, or existing migrations.
- Do not delete, overwrite, or regenerate existing migrations; they must remain intact for production database updates.
- For any database structure or seed-data change in `libraries/*/hedhog/table/*.yaml` or `libraries/*/hedhog/data/*.yaml`, create a new SQL migration under `apps/api/prisma/migrations` that mirrors the YAML change.
- After creating a new migration file, apply it by running `pnpm prisma:deploy` from `apps/api`.
- For DB-driven schema refreshes use `pnpm db:update` only when applicable after the database has the expected structure.
- Do not use `prisma migrate dev` as the repository workflow unless a future explicit project policy replaces this one.

2. Library bootstrap workflow.
- For new libraries use `hedhog dev create-library --name <library>`.
- After creation always run `pnpm install` at repository root.

3. Admin asset sync workflow.
- If files change under:
  - `apps/admin/src/app/(app)/(libraries)`
  - `apps/admin/messages`
- Run `hedhog dev assets-to-library <library...>` before finishing.

## Task-to-Agent Routing Matrix

Use specialized agents by intent and file area.

| Task intent | Primary paths | Agent |
| --- | --- | --- |
| End-to-end dashboard work: widget lifecycle, component catalog, layout/position behavior, dashboard UX and backend integration | `apps/admin/src/app/(app)/(libraries)/*/dashboard/**`, `libraries/*/src/dashboard/**`, `libraries/*/hedhog/frontend/widgets/**` | `Dashboard` |
| NestJS backend modules, controllers, services, DTOs, endpoint behavior | `libraries/*/src`, `apps/api/src` | `Backend` |
| Route permission seeds and role synchronization for endpoints | `libraries/*/hedhog/data/route.yaml`, `libraries/*/hedhog/data/role.yaml` | `Route Permissions` |
| Library design, cross-library entity reuse, dependency ordering, library bootstrap | `libraries/*/package.json`, `libraries/*/hedhog/` | `Hedhog Library` |
| Writing/editing hedhog table YAML and data seed YAML (columns, FKs, locale, seeds, relations) | `libraries/*/hedhog/table/*.yaml`, `libraries/*/hedhog/data/*.yaml` (non-permission) | `Hedhog YAML` |
| Next.js pages/components/forms/data layer/i18n UX | `apps/admin/**` | `Frontend` |
| Library README/API+DB documentation generation or refresh | `libraries/*/README.md` and module docs | `Library Documentation` |

## Delegation Rules

1. Always pick the narrowest responsible agent.
2. Dashboard-first by intent: if the request is about dashboard behavior/features, `Dashboard` is the primary owner even when touched files include non-dashboard paths.
3. For multi-domain tasks, sequence agents by dependency:
- dashboard-first work (`Dashboard`) -> route permissions (`Route Permissions`) when endpoint surface changes; otherwise for non-dashboard work: library design (`Hedhog Library`) -> YAML authoring (`Hedhog YAML`) -> backend runtime (`Backend`) -> route permissions (`Route Permissions`) -> frontend (`Frontend`) -> documentation (`Library Documentation`)
4. Do not keep domain-specific playbooks in this file.
5. If an endpoint changes, route permission synchronization is mandatory via `Route Permissions` ownership.
6. `Hedhog Library` decides table structure; `Hedhog YAML` writes the actual YAML files. Never conflate these two responsibilities.
7. For dashboard widget catalog and role seeds (`dashboard_component.yaml`, `dashboard_component_role.yaml`), write in the target library path (`libraries/<library>/hedhog/data`), populate `library_slug` with the owning library, and do not edit core equivalents unless the request explicitly targets core.
8. When dashboard widget templates are copied into admin runtime assets, assume the generated file name is prefixed as `<library>.<widget>` and resolve the React component using `library_slug + '.' + slug`.

## Completion Checklist

- Correct agent(s) selected for each touched concern.
- Mandatory workflows respected (Prisma, library bootstrap, asset sync).
- No cross-workspace deep relative imports introduced.
- Focused validation executed when feasible.
